title: Magnetic phase transition composite material
abstract: A phase transition composite material (1) includes a dispersion of single-domain magnetic nanoparticles (2) and an elastic encapsulant (3) arranged to separate the nanoparticles (2). The composite material (1) is deformable between first and second states. In the first state the material is relaxed and in the second state the material is stressed. In the first state, the magnetizations of the magnetic nanoparticles (2) are less ordered (e.g., the magnetic nanoparticles (2) exhibit superparamagnetism) and, in the second state, the magnetizations of the magnetic nanoparticles (2) are more ordered (e.g., the magnetic nanoparticles (2) exhibit superferromagnetism or superantiferromagnetism).
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A phase transition composition comprising: a dispersion of single-domain magnetic nanoparticles; and an elastic encapsulant arranged to encapsulate and separate the single domain magnetic nanoparticles, wherein: the phase transition composition is deformable between a first state and a second state; in the first state, the phase transition composition is relatively relaxed and in the second state the phase transition composition is relatively stressed or in the first state the phase transition composition is relatively stressed and in the second state the phase transition composition is relatively relaxed; and the magnetizations of the single-domain magnetic nanoparticles in the second state are more ordered than the magnetizations of the single-domain magnetic nanoparticles in the first state. 2. The phase transition composition of claim l, wherein a phase of the single-domain magnetic nanoparticles in the first state comprises a superparamagnetic phase and the phase of the single-domain magnetic nanoparticles in the second state comprises a superferromagnetic phase. 3. The phase transition composition of claim l or claim 2, wherein in the second state the magnetizations of the magnetic nanoparticles are at least partially aligned or at least partially anti-aligned. 4. The phase transition composition of any one of claims l to 3, wherein in the second state the magnetizations of the single-domain magnetic nanoparticles are aligned or substantially aligned or anti-aligned or substantially anti-aligned. 5. The phase transition composition of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein in the first state the magnetizations of the single-domain magnetic nanoparticles are randomly orientated. 6. The phase transition composition of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the elastic encapsulant comprises a plurality of elastic encapsulants and the magnetic nanoparticles are each partially or completely coated by a one of the plurality of elastic encapsulants to form a plurality of encapsulated single-domain magnetic nanoparticles. 7. The phase transition composition of claim 6, wherein the elastic encapsulent comprises an encapsulent material and an elastic material. 8. The phase transition composition of claim 6 or claim 7, wherein each of the plurality of encapsulated single-domain magnetic nanoparticles is partially or completely coated by an antiadhesive. 9. The phase transition composition of claim 8, wherein the antiadhesive comprises at least one of a polymer, a fluoropolymer, a polyolefin and a polystyrene. 10. The phase transition composition of claim 8 or claim 9, wherein the antiadhesive has a thickness of between about 0.5 nm and about 5 nm. 11. The phase transition composition of any one of claims 6 to 10, wherein the elastic encapsulant has a thickness of between about 1 nm and about 10 nm. 12. The phase transition composition of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the elastic encapsulant comprises a matrix and the single-domain magnetic nanoparticles are embedded in the matrix. 13. The phase transition composition of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein the elastic encapsulant comprises at least one of a polymer, an elastomer or a polybutadiene. 14. The phase transition composition of any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein the single-domain magnetic nanoparticles comprise one of a ferromagnetic material, a ferrimagnetic material or an antiferromagnetic material. 15. The phase transition composition of any one of claims 1 to 14, wherein the single-domain magnetic nanoparticles have a thickness between about 3 nm and about 50 nm. 16. The phase transition composition of any one of claims 1 to 15, further comprising: interstitial voids between the single-domain magnetic nanoparticles. 17. A heat pump, comprising: a block of the phase transition composition according to any one of claims l to 16; an actuator assembly for reversibly deforming the block of the phase transition composition; and a heat exchanger comprising a fluid channel in contact with and/or passing through the block of the composite material, wherein the fluid channel contains a working fluid providing thermal communication between the block of the phase transition composition and the heat exchanger. 18. A method of operating a heat pump, comprising: reversibly deforming a block of the phase transition composition of any one of claims 1 to 16 such that the phase transition composition passes between the first state and the second state; and passing a working fluid through a fluid channel in thermal communication with the block of the phase transition composition to extract heat from the block of the phase transition composition. 19. A method of forming a phase transition composition, the method comprising: providing single-domain magnetic nanoparticles; providing an elastic encapsulant; and arranging the single-domain magnetic nanoparticles and the elastic encapsulant to form a dispersion of the single-domain magnetic nanoparticles, wherein the elastic encapsulant separates the single-domain magnetic nanoparticles, and wherein the phase transition composition is configured to transition under compression from a first state where the single-domain magnetic nanoparticles are separated by distances providing that the single-domain magnetic nanoparticles are in a superparamagnetic 

 phase and a second state where the single-domain magnetic nanoparticles are separated by distances providing that the single-domain magnetic nanoparticles are in a superferromagnetic phase. 20. The method of claim 19, comprising: providing polymer chains; functionalizing each polymer chain with a first functional group, wherein the first functional group is configured to bind to a surface of the single-domain magnetic nanoparticles; attaching the polymer chains to the single-domain magnetic nanoparticles via the first binding group; and precipitating and/ or cross-linking the polymer chains attached to the singledomain magnetic nanoparticles. 21. The method of claim 20, further comprising: functionalising each polymer chain with a second functional group, wherein the second functional group comprises an antiadhesive, and wherein the second functional group is provided at a different end of the polymer chain from the first functional group.
